patterns.name: Over-commenting: comment restates code
patterns.description: Comment restates what the next line of code already expresses clearly.
patterns.remediation: Remove comments that merely describe the mechanics; only keep comments explaining why.

patterns.name: Empty catch block
patterns.description: Catch block with no body silently swallows exceptions, hiding errors.
patterns.remediation: At minimum, log or rethrow the error, or add a comment explaining why silent handling is intentional.

patterns.name: Placeholder TODO/FIXME left in code
patterns.description: TODO/FIXME/HACK/XXX marker was left in the code without being resolved.
patterns.remediation: Resolve the underlying issue or track it in an issue tracker and remove the inline marker.

patterns.name: Commented-out code block
patterns.description: Commented-out code left in the file instead of being deleted.
patterns.remediation: Delete unused code; version control preserves history if the code is ever needed again.

patterns.name: Debug console.log left behind
patterns.description: Debug console statement was left in production code after development.
patterns.remediation: Remove debug logging statements or replace with a structured logger that can be toggled by environment.

patterns.name: Unnecessary else after return
patterns.description: An else branch follows a return statement, making the else redundant and adding nesting.
patterns.remediation: Remove the else keyword; code after the if block is only reached when the condition is false.

patterns.name: Unnecessary single-expression wrapper function
patterns.description: Function body does nothing but delegate to another function with the same arguments.
patterns.remediation: Replace the wrapper with a direct reference or alias to the wrapped function.

patterns.name: Try-catch around obviously infallible code
patterns.description: Try-catch wraps code that cannot throw, adding defensive ceremony without benefit.
patterns.remediation: Remove the try-catch wrapper from synchronous operations that provably cannot throw.

patterns.name: Obvious type annotation on literal
patterns.description: Type annotation on a variable whose type is obvious from the literal value assigned.
patterns.remediation: Omit the type annotation and rely on TypeScript's inference for obvious literal assignments.

patterns.name: Async function with no await
patterns.description: Function is declared async but contains no await expression, wrapping the return value unnecessarily.
patterns.remediation: Remove the async keyword if the function does not need to await any asynchronous operation.

patterns.name: Barrel file that only re-exports
patterns.description: Index or barrel file that solely re-exports from other modules without adding any logic.
patterns.remediation: Consider whether the barrel indirection is needed; deep imports are often clearer and faster to compile.

patterns.name: Null check on provably non-null value
patterns.description: Null guard on a value that the type system or prior logic guarantees is non-null.
patterns.remediation: Remove the guard or add a TypeScript non-null assertion if you are certain the value cannot be null.

patterns.name: Function with empty or comment-only body
patterns.description: Function body is empty or contains only a comment, providing no implementation.
patterns.remediation: Implement the function body or remove the function if it is no longer needed.

patterns.name: Redundant explicit return type annotation
patterns.description: Return type annotation duplicates what TypeScript would infer automatically.
patterns.remediation: Remove the explicit return type annotation when TypeScript can infer it from the function body.

patterns.name: Verbose manual property copy instead of spread
patterns.description: Object is constructed by manually copying multiple properties from another object.
patterns.remediation: Use object spread (`{ ...source }`) or `Object.assign` to copy object properties concisely.
